figures.caption: FIG. 8. (a) Spectra |m̃a(ω)| for a = x, y, z in Néel phase where two-color laser pulse with C3 symmetry around z axis is applied. (b) Spectra |m̃a(ω)| for a = x, y, z in canted phase where two-color laser pulse with C3 symmetry around z axis is applied. We set the static magnetic-field strength B to (a) B/J = 0.1 and (b) B/J = 3.0. We also set Bac/J = 0.1 in both cases. The laser frequencies Ω1 and Ω2 are chosen to be the magnetic resonance values: ℏΩ1/J = ϵNéel,β

FIG. 12. Comparison of harmonic generation spectra with system sizes, 10 × 10, 20 × 20, and 30 × 30. Panels (a), (b), and (c) show the spectrum |m̃x(ω)| in the Néel, canted, and WF phases, respectively. We set the static magnetic field strength to B/J = (a) 0.5, (b) 3.0, and (c) 0.5. We apply the laser pulse field is parallel to the x axis to the system. The horizontal axes are plotted in unit of Ωn (n = 1, 2, 3). The unit Ωn denotes the resonant frequency in each phase given by ℏΩ1/J = ϵNéel,β

FIG. 16. Spectra |m̃x(ω)| in Néel phase when Bac ∥ ez with or without static magnetic field B. We compare the cases with Bac/J = 0.0, 0.1, 2.0. We set B/J = 3.0 in the left column and B/J = 0 in the right column. In the presence of B, no dynamical symmetry exists and every harmonic generation can potentially appear. By contrast, in the absence of B, dynamical symmetry forbids every harmonic generation. We set the laser-pulse frequency Ω so that it equals to the magnon gap of the β mode, ϵNéel,β

FIG. 17. (a) Harmonic generation spectra |m̃a(ω)| for a = x, y, z in canted phase when laser field is parallel to z axis and extremely strong. We set Bac/J = 3.0. The laser frequency Ω is chosen to be the magnon gap of the α mode: ℏΩ = ϵCant,α

FIG. 5. Selection rules of magnon harmonic generation in Néel phase from viewpoint of angular momentum conservation. We consider the case where linearly polarized THz laser with Bac ∥ êx [see panels (c)-(e) in Fig. 4]. Panels (a) and (c) show the resonant absorption of photons. A single photon with energy ℏΩ creates one magnon when ℏΩ = ϵNéel,β

FIG. 15. (a) Schematic image of precession motion in αmode magnetic resonance of canted phase. Symbols m1 and m2 denote the magnetic moments of A and B sublattices, respectively, while M = (m1 + m2)/2 is the uniform magnetization. Light-colored arrows mean the directions of magnetic moments in the equilibrium state. The sublattice moments draw a ellipse orbital in the coordinate (Sξ r, Sη r , Sζ r), in which the axis Sξ r is the major axis and the axis Sη r is the minor axis. The uniform magnetization M also has an ellipse orbital whose major and minor axes are respectively the Sx and Sy axes. Panels (b) and (c) show how the first harmonic generation peaks |m̃a(Ω)| (a = x, y) depend on the static (dc) magnetic field B when (b) Bac ∥ ex or (c) Bac ∥ ey. Panels (d) and (e) show how the third harmonic generation peaks |m̃a(3Ω)| (a = x, y) depend on the static magnetic field B when (d) Bac ∥ ex or (e) Bac ∥ ey. We set Bac/J = 0.01 for upper panels (b) and (c) and Bac/J = 0.05 for lower panels.
FIG. 14. B/J dependence of ratio Y in WF phase. We set D/J = 0.05.

FIG. 9. (a) Spectra |m̃a(ω)| for a = x, y, z in Néel phase where two-color laser pulse with C4 symmetry around z axis is applied. (b) Spectra |m̃a(ω)| for a = x, y, z in canted phase where two-color laser pulse with C4 symmetry around z axis is applied. We set the magnetic-field strength B to (a) B/J = 0.1 and (b) B/J = 3.0. We also set Bac/J = 0.1 in both cases. The laser frequencies Ω1 and Ω2 are respectively given by ℏΩ1/J = ϵNéel,β

FIG. 10. Diamond symbols in panels (a), (b), and (c) show Bac dependence of |m̃x(Ω)|, |m̃z(2Ω)|, and |m̃x(3Ω)|, respectively, in the Néel phase with static magnetic field B/J = 0.5 when we apply the one-color laser pulse oscillating in the x axis. We set the laser frequency Ω to the resonant value: ℏΩ = ϵNéel,β

FIG. 7. Numerical result of harmonic generation in WF phase at kBT/J = 10−3. (a) Spectra |m̃x(ω)| of model (2.1) in WF phase. The first, second, and third rows show spectra a = x, y, and z, respectively. The first, second, and third columns show spectra when the applied laser pulse is parallel to the x, y, and z axis, respectively. The symmetry operation below each column [such as (T̂xÛz(π), Tac/2)] denotes the dynamical symmetry in each setup. We set B/J = 0.5 and Bac/J = 0.1. The photon energies ℏΩ1 = 0.591J and ℏΩ2 = 0.374J are set to the gaps of the α-mode magnon (ϵWF,α

FIG. 4. Numerical result of harmonic generation in Néel phase at kBT/J = 10−3. Panels (a), (b) show plots of the amplitude of the Fourier transform m̃(ω) = (m̃x(ω), m̃y(ω), m̃z(ω)) of the model (2.1) in the Néel phase vs. the normalized frequency ω/Ω. The static magnetic field B is on (B/J = 0.5) in the panel (a) and off (B/J = 0.0) in the panel (b). We set Bac/J = 0.1 in both panels. The laser frequency Ω = 2π/Tac is set to the resonant frequency of magnon: Ω = Ω1 (Ω = Ω2) in the presence (absence, respectively) of the magnetic field. Ω1 and Ω2 are given by ℏΩ1 = ϵNéel,β

FIG. 11. Spectrum intensity |m̃x(Ω)| of first harmonic generation by one-color laser pulse along x axis. We plot |m̃x(Ω)| as a function of laser frequency Ω and strength of ac magnetic field Bac. The dashed line shows the resonant frequency ϵNéel,β
FIG. 13. B/J dependence of ratio Y in canted phase. We set K/J = 0.2.

FIG. 6. Numerical result of harmonic generation in canted phase at kBT/J = 10−3. (a) Spectra |m̃a(ω)| of model (2.1) in canted phase, where the spins of the thermal state are chosen to be in Sx-Sz plane. The first, second, and third rows show spectra a = x, y, and z, respectively. The first, second, and third columns show spectra when the applied laser pulse is parallel to the x, y, and z axis, respectively. The symmetry operation below each column [such as (T̂xÛz(π), Tac/2)] denotes the dynamical symmetry in each setup. We set B/J = 3.0, Bac/J = 0.1, and the photon energy ℏΩ = 2.7J , that is equivalent to the gap of the α-mode magnon, ϵCant,α
